title: Combat Damage in Vacuum
summary: How combat damage affects ships in the vacuum of space.
details: Hull breaches cause explosive decompression. Crew in breached sections die without suits. Atmosphere vents until equalized or sealed. Debris from impacts becomes secondary projectiles inside ship. No fire without oxygen - but hot components remain hazardous. Damaged systems may vent fluids that freeze instantly. Electrical arcs in vacuum behave differently. All crew should maintain pressure suits during combat.
annotations: Space is unforgiving of hull breaches.
Damage control is life or death, literally.
